name: Speed
description: Every character and monster has a speed, which is the distance in feet the creature can walk in 1 round. This number assumes short bursts of energetic movement in the midst of a life-threatening situation.
effects: On your turn, you can move a distance up to your speed
You can break up your movement, using some before and after your action
You can move through a nonhostile creature's space
You can't willingly end your move in a space occupied by another creature
Moving through a hostile creature's space counts as Difficult Terrain
A creature can squeeze through a space one size smaller than it; squeezing costs 1 extra foot per foot of movement

name: Difficult Terrain
description: Combat rarely takes place in bare rooms or on featureless plains. Boulder-strewn caverns, briar-choked forests, treacherous staircases -- the setting of a typical fight contains difficult terrain.
effects: Every foot of movement in difficult terrain costs 1 extra foot
This rule applies whether the creature is walking, climbing, swimming, or flying
Multiple sources of difficult terrain do not stack; terrain is either difficult or it is not
The spaces of other creatures count as difficult terrain whether they are hostile or not
Low furniture, rubble, undergrowth, steep stairs, snow, and shallow bogs are examples

name: Jumping
description: Your Strength determines how far you can jump. You can make a Long Jump or a High Jump as part of your movement.
effects: Long Jump: With a 10-foot running start, jump a number of feet equal to your Strength score; standing long jump is half that
High Jump: With a 10-foot running start, jump a number of feet equal to 3 + your Strength modifier (minimum 0 feet); standing high jump is half that
Each foot you jump costs a foot of movement
In some circumstances, the DM might allow a Strength (Athletics) check to jump farther than normal
You can extend your arms half your height above yourself during a high jump to reach above

name: Climbing
description: While climbing, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot (2 extra feet in difficult terrain), unless a creature has a climbing speed.
effects: Each foot of climbing costs 1 extra foot of movement (2 extra in difficult terrain)
A creature with a climbing speed can climb at its full climbing speed without extra cost
The DM might require a Strength (Athletics) check to climb a slippery vertical surface or one with few handholds
At the DM's option, climbing a slippery or precarious surface requires a successful DC 15 Strength (Athletics) check

name: Swimming
description: While swimming, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot (2 extra feet in difficult terrain), unless a creature has a swimming speed.
effects: Each foot of swimming costs 1 extra foot of movement (2 extra in difficult terrain)
A creature with a swimming speed can swim at its full swimming speed without extra cost
The DM might require a Strength (Athletics) check in rough water or strong currents
A creature that runs out of breath or is choking can survive for a number of rounds equal to its Constitution modifier (minimum 1 round) before it drops to 0 HP

name: Flying
description: Flying creatures enjoy many benefits of mobility, but they must also deal with the danger of falling. If a flying creature is knocked Prone, has its speed reduced to 0, or is otherwise deprived of the ability to move, the creature falls.
effects: A flying creature can move in any direction, including vertically
If a flying creature's speed is reduced to 0 while flying, it falls
If a flying creature is knocked Prone while flying, it falls
A creature with a flying speed can hover if its stat block says so; otherwise, it must keep moving to stay aloft
A falling creature descends up to 500 feet per round (at the end of its turn)
Fall damage: 1d6 Bludgeoning damage per 10 feet fallen, to a maximum of 20d6

name: Falling
description: A fall from a great height is one of the most common hazards facing an adventurer. At the end of a fall, a creature takes 1d6 Bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it fell, to a maximum of 20d6.
effects: 1d6 Bludgeoning damage per 10 feet fallen
Maximum of 20d6 damage (200 feet)
The creature lands Prone unless it avoids taking damage from the fall
A creature falls 500 feet instantly at the end of its turn; if the fall is greater than 500 feet, the remaining distance is fallen at the start of its next turn

name: Suffocating
description: A creature can hold its breath for a number of minutes equal to 1 + its Constitution modifier (minimum 30 seconds). When a creature runs out of breath, it can survive for a number of rounds equal to its Constitution modifier (minimum 1 round). At the start of its next turn after that, it drops to 0 Hit Points and is dying.
effects: Hold breath for 1 + Constitution modifier minutes (minimum 30 seconds)
After running out of breath, survive for a number of rounds equal to Constitution modifier (minimum 1 round)
After those rounds, the creature drops to 0 HP and is dying, and can't regain HP until it can breathe again
A creature that can't breathe takes damage at the start of each of its turns if already at 0 HP

name: Travel Pace
description: While traveling, a group of adventurers can move at a normal, fast, or slow pace. A fast pace makes characters less perceptive, while a slow pace allows for stealth.
effects: Fast Pace: 400 feet per minute, 4 miles per hour, 30 miles per day; -5 penalty to passive Perception
Normal Pace: 300 feet per minute, 3 miles per hour, 24 miles per day; no modifiers
Slow Pace: 200 feet per minute, 2 miles per hour, 18 miles per day; able to use Stealth
Forced March: After 8 hours of travel, DC 10 Constitution saving throw each additional hour or gain 1 level of Exhaustion (DC increases by 1 per hour)
